calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Leon Ainer for Irene Dillard.

The Court stated that there is an issue with Attorney's costs that don't seem to line up with hearing dates and service fees that the Court is having trouble matching up to the register of actions. The Court also raised the issue of the accounting not reflecting the debts shown in the final settlement statement and stated that the cash on hand of $278,000.00 as of April 2019 doesn't seem to have been deposited into an interest-bearing account. Mr. Ainer stated that all of the e-file charges are costs related to this estate, that the only thing he can think of is that there are a few transactions that were rejected and re-filed, that he estimates those costs to be about $40.00 and that if the Court wants, he will make an adjustment. Mr. Ainer also stated that he does not know why the final settlement statement doesn't reconcile, that he compared the items on both documents and that can't tell by the Probate Examiner's notes what item doesn't reconcile. In addition, Mr. Ainer stated that they had mediation in December, that all of the objections were discussed and settled, that his Client did not realize that the estate account wasn't interest-bearing until they did the accounting and by then it was too late, and that he worked really hard to close this estate. The Amended Report on First and Final Account and Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-27-2020 Continued To Feb-26-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-07-2020 Continued To Jan-29-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Continued To Jan-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Leon Ainer for Irene Dillard.
Giulio Accornero for American Research Bureau.

Mr. Accornero stated that some progress has been made and that this is an unusual case with an issue of whether $200,000.00 in equity in the estate was saved. Mr. Accornero also stated that he has requested some informal discovery, that he has received some answers, but it raises several questions and that it is best to continue the matter and have a referral to mediation. Mr. Ainer stated that he's not sure that makes sense, that if there are requests for relief sought, he's not sure what the requests are, that there was no notice of proposed action and that there is the issue of the account not being in an interest bearing account. The Court noted that the deep issue is that the loan should not have been taken and that it was a breach of duty. Mr. Ainer stated that the estate is rather small, that the notice of trustee sale had occurred well before the Decedent's death and that he has no idea why the Decedent did not pay his mortgage. Mr. Ainer also stated that the only account the Decedent had was a pay on death account, that the notice of trustee sale had already expired, that the Administrator received her letters on October 18, 2017 and that the property was due for sale on October 28th. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than December 13, 2019. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by December 18, 2019. The Amended Report on First and Final Account, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on January 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-13-2019 Continued To Sep-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Leon A. Ainer for Irene Dillard. Giulion Accornero for American Research Bureau (Objector).

Mr. Accornero confirmed for the Court that the original objections stand. The Court stated that the declaration needed to contain verified information from the fiduciary and inquired if the parties had met and conferred to resolve the issues. Mr. Ainer stated that they have not talked, that counsel was on vacation, and that they had responded to the probate examiner's notes. Mr. Accornero stated that there are complex issues, that there is a problem with communication, and that there's been no communication at all. The Court stated that the first step would be for counsel to talk to each other and then determine if they want a mediator. Mr. Accornero stated that they've have a petition before the Court and the Court has discretion to proceed. The Court stated that the responses aren't properly before the Court, that it can't consider that information today, and that both sides would need to ask the Court to proceed. Mr. Ainer addressed the issues that include notice not being prepared for the initial loan and that the proceeds from the sale of the real property didn't accrue interest. The Court stated that the merits say that the loan never should have been made, that it cost the estate/Mr. Accornero's clients, and that he is entitled to litigate the matter. Mr. Ainer stated that there are no facts to find. The Court stated that it does not have Mr. Ainer's responses properly before it and that the responses are not verified by Mr. Ainer's client. Mr. Ainer stated that he would have his client vrify the filing and that he had concerns about litigation over two issues. The Court noted that they did not have Annie's consent to the loan. Mr. Ainer stated that Annie was unavailable. Brief discussions regarding a further hearing date are held. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court continued the Amended Report on First and Final Account and Petition for Final Distribution and for Allowance of Compensation to Attorneys and Administrator for Ordinary Services and for Attorneys Extraordinary Services to September 24, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that by that date counsel should see what they can accomplish, figure out what's still at issue, discuss mediation, and or be ready to inform the Court if they want it to rule on a discreet set of issues.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court noted that only a partial Inventory and Appraisal has been filed which indicates that bond needs to be increased. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator and the Attorney to appear on April 11, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear, why a final Inventory and Appraisal has not been filed, and why the bond issue has not been addressed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-12-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Leon Ainer for Irene Dillard.

Mr. Ainer stated that he filed a Substitution of Attorney yesterday, that he is in the process of working with Philips Bonding Company to get his Client bonded and that the nomination was also filed yesterday. Mr. Ainer also stated that Ms. Dillard was not able to speak with the Nieces, but was able to confirm their last known address with family, that she has no phone number or fax number for the Nieces, that his Client has not met with the family in thirty years, that he believes the other family members she met with had contact with the Nieces less than seven years ago and that his Client has not received any mail back. In addition, Mr. Ainer stated that the sole asset of the Estate is in San Francisco, but is subject to a Trustee sale at the end of the month (October 30th), that his Client received notification from a Lender indicating that they are willing to lend funds to redeem the foreclosure amount and that having limited authority may cause a problem. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with limited powers and bond in the amount of $450.00 (subject to review of the order). The Court will take the issue full authority under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Wu. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-20-2017 Continued To Oct-11-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
